name: Separator
description: A thin visual divider between content groups. Built on Base UI Separator with StyleX styling.

Root.description: A 1px line that adapts to its orientation. Uses colorBorderMuted to match in-component dividers (Menu, Sidebar).

orientation.description: Direction of the separator. Vertical separators stretch to the height of their flex parent.

decorative.description: When true, the separator is presentational only and exposes role="none" to assistive technology.

intents.reasoning: Separator is the canonical 1px divider for splitting content groups, settings sections, or list groups.
Use orientation="vertical" inside a flex row to separate inline metadata or toolbar groups.
When the divider is purely visual and adjacent content is already grouped semantically, mark it decorative so screen readers skip it.

avoidWhen.scenario: Replacing a heading or section landmark
avoidWhen.reasoning: Separator is decoration, not structure. Use a heading or landmark for semantic grouping.

avoidWhen.scenario: As a card or panel border
avoidWhen.reasoning: Containers should own their borders. Adding a Separator inside duplicates the visual.
Those components ship their own Separator part wired to their roving focus and ARIA model.

examples.name: horizontal
examples.description: Default horizontal divider between two stacked sections

examples.name: vertical
examples.description: Vertical divider between inline metadata in a flex row
examples.code: Author 5 min read

examples.name: decorative
examples.description: Decorative variant for purely visual splits
